name: Gifu Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the abundant forests and mountains of Gifu

description: A white field charged at the centre with a green circular emblem stylising the kanji 岐 of the prefecture's name.
history: Gifu's prefectural symbol was adopted on 25 August 1932. The flag, with the green emblem on a white field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same period.

features.symbolism: purity, peace and the bright future of Gifu

a stylised abstraction of the kanji 岐 (gi) of 岐阜 (Gifu), enclosed in a circle representing harmony among the prefecture's people; the green colour symbolises the abundant nature of the mountainous prefecture.
